claim: The proof has been reduced to a single KKT barrier/uniqueness lemma; hidden-sector and boundary accounting are certified or localized, but the global theorem is not yet proved.
claim_boundary: This is a proof-state certificate, not a proof of Talagrand.

lemma_ladder.content: Lambda_hc(m) lies in ker(Act) for all 2048 residue masks, so sector-33 height corrections do not change the Act-dependent shell functional.
lemma_ladder.proof_role: removes hidden finite obstruction from Talagrand

lemma_ladder.content: Talagrand shell domination is reduced to the Act-visible Golay shell incidence channel.
lemma_ladder.proof_role: moves problem from A985 transport to shell polynomial / entropy problem

lemma_ladder.content: Small and medium support faces are bounded by the AM-GM edge-count sieve.
High-support contained block counts depend only on complement size; equal-on-support has strict negative tangent Hessian and subunit proper-boundary ratio.
lemma_ladder.proof_role: reduces high boundary to global-on-face uniqueness

lemma_ladder.content: External fields can have local eta above 2/w far below the ratio barrier.
Every positive interior maximizer is equal; high-local-eta regions cannot reach ratio one.

final_open_lemma.name: KKT barrier uniqueness lemma
final_open_lemma.statement: For w=12,16, the Act-visible shell objective has no non-equal positive interior maximizer; equivalently, any point with shell ratio at or above one is forced to the equal point by the KKT equations.
final_open_lemma.why_not_done: Current evidence is numerical/local/certificate-reduction. A rigorous global barrier or SOS certificate is still missing.
final_open_lemma.why_this_closes: Boundary faces are already controlled; if every positive interior maximizer is equal, then the global maximum is the equal vector and the Talagrand shell inequality follows.

theorem_state.name: Talagrand uniqueness argument state
theorem_state.next_actual_breakthrough_target: Produce a rigorous barrier certificate: high eta implies ratio <= c<1, while ratio near 1 implies negative-definite KKT contraction to the equal point.
what_is_solved: The hidden sector-33/action-return obstruction is closed.
The problem is reduced to the Act-visible shell polynomial.
Small and medium boundary strata are rigorously bounded.
High-support boundary strata are reduced by complement size and strict local maximality.
The naive all-external-field SDPI route is ruled out.
theorem_state.wrong_route_removed: Uniform external-field SDPI with coefficient 2/w is false; proof must be barrier/KKT, not global spectral independence.
